# Feature-Sliced Design (FSD)

_Applies when: building or modifying any React / React Native frontend in this org._

Architecture rules for all frontend work. Enforce these layer boundaries — they are ESLint errors (`eslint-plugin-boundaries`, zero warnings), not suggestions.

**But the gate only exists if it is wired.** `boundaries` silently drops every import it cannot resolve to a file instead of reporting it — the `checkAllOrigins`/`checkUnknownLocals` pre-gate (both default `false`) runs before the rule evaluates, so it fails **open**: green, at severity `error`.

- It needs `settings["import/resolver"].typescript` (`eslint-import-resolver-typescript`; it reads tsconfig `paths` on its own — no consumer `baseUrl` required). The `node` resolver is NOT enough — it can't read `paths`, so aliased imports stay unchecked while only relative ones error. Path aliases + no TS resolver = the plugin is a **100% no-op**.
- The resolver must be a **direct devDependency of every app** — `eslint-module-utils` resolves it relative to the linted source file, so under pnpm's isolated layout inheriting it transitively from a shared preset does not resolve.
- `lint` green proves FSD conformance **only** if a fixture with an *aliased* illegal import actually errors. Keep one, and verify it fails before trusting the gate — validating with a relative-import fixture is a false victory.
- A gate-bearing rule also fails open by **deprecation**: deprecation notices are not lint findings (absent from the exit code and the warning counts), so a rule left at `error` can silently vanish at a routine major — `eslint-plugin-boundaries@6` deprecates `no-private`/`entry-point`, dropped in v7, which would erase half the layer gates. Pin the major and ticket any deprecated rule that guards a boundary.

## Layers (imports flow down, never up or sideways)

`app` → `routes` → `pages` → `widgets` → `features` → `entities` → `shared`

- A layer imports only from layers **below** it. Never upward, never sideways between slices of the same layer.
- `shared` imports nothing project-specific. Cross-cutting types and the UI kit live here.
- Path aliases per layer: `@app/*` `@routes/*` `@pages/*` `@widgets/*` `@features/*` `@entities/*` `@shared/*`. No deep relative imports (`../../../`).

## Monorepo layout

- `apps/*` — `web`, `api`, `mobile` (each its own FSD tree). `packages/*` — `shared-types` (Zod SSOT), `shared-ui`, `shared-api`, `config` (shared ESLint/Prettier/Tailwind presets).
- Types are defined ONCE in `@repo/shared-types` and imported by web/api/mobile simultaneously — never duplicated in an app.
- Dependency order for changes: `shared-types` → `api` → `web`/`mobile`.

## Slice structure

Each slice exposes a public API via `index.ts` (barrel). Import across slices ONLY through that public API — never reach into internals (`features/x/model/...`).

```
features/
  auth/
    index.ts   # public API — the only legal import surface
    model/     # state, stores, business logic
    ui/        # components
    api/       # requests, query options
    lib/       # slice-local helpers
```

## Conventions

- Server-state via TanStack Query; keep `queryOptions()` factories in the slice's `api/` (see `15-frontend.md`).
- Routes (`routes/`) stay thin and delegate to `pages/`; logic lives in pages, not routes.
- New behavior starts as a `feature`; promote to `widget` only when it composes multiple features for a layout region.
- No hardcoded values — config goes in `shared/config/`.

## Review checklist

- No upward, sideways, or cross-slice-internal imports.
- Every slice has an `index.ts` public API.
- Business logic sits in `model/`, not in `ui/` components.
- Shared types come from `@repo/shared-types`, not re-declared locally.
